mechanism: Widening HY-IG credit spreads signal deteriorating credit conditions and rising default risk, which historically precede equity drawdowns. The HMM regime model detects stress states from spread dynamics.
key_finding: HMM regime detection wins; drawdown avoidance, not alpha
caveats: HMM states are estimated in-sample and may not persist out-of-sample
Credit spread data begins 2000 — limited to ~25 years of history
Strategy reduces drawdowns but does not generate excess return over buy-and-hold
